funk.rationale: Inferred from inventory (both white and dark Jamaican rum stocked Ã¢ÂÂ ester-forward category) and Smokey the Pear v2 explicitly preferring white Jamaican rum as sub-base for ester complementarity with pear.

savory_saline.rationale: No strong evidence of preference or aversion in documented originals or inventory. Worth exploring.

background.profession: Vice President of Decision Sciences. Leads an 8-9 person team of PhDs specializing in predictive modeling (supervised and unsupervised learning). Physics PhD. Strong applied background in financial modeling for investment predictions.
background.typical_context: Solo evenings (contemplative, often spirit-forward); entertaining friends and family; occasional themed drinks for specific guests (e.g., habanero-loving Dave).
background.household_context: Wife is Taiwanese -- household has access to many Asian ingredients (yuzu, shiso, sake, mirin, matcha, Asian pear, dried plums, oolong, jasmine tea, black sesame potentially available; specifics still TBD). When designing drinks, consider whether these ingredients would unlock distinctive builds and ask if relevant.
background.other_notes: Maintains an organized, well-stocked bar with particular depth in whisk(e)y (5 regional styles), bitters (10 SKUs), and an interesting fortified wine and liqueur collection. Keeps an active shopping list and is interested in highest-impact-per-dollar additions. Has a documented set of original cocktails reflecting a clear creative voice.

archetypes.name: The Experimenter
archetypes.description: New ingredients, unusual techniques, willing to risk a miss.

archetypes.name: The Host
archetypes.description: Crowd-pleasers, batched builds, easy to serve at scale.

archetypes.name: Sophisticated
archetypes.description: Refined palate, premium spirits, elevated presentation.

archetypes.name: Curious / Studious
archetypes.description: Deep dive into history, technique, and ingredient provenance.

archetypes.name: Bartender's Bartender
archetypes.description: Industry respect, precise technique, knows all the classics.

archetypes.name: Spirit-forward / Old-School
archetypes.description: Spirit does the talking — minimal dilution, strong builds.

archetype_notes: Glenn has the discipline of a classicist with the curiosity of a modernist. Not frou-frou, not theatrical, not minimalist. Treats cocktail-making the way he probably treats predictive modeling Ã¢ÂÂ structural, evidence-based, iterative, and willing to reject results that don't hold up.

disliked_ingredients.name: Chartreuse
disliked_ingredients.notes: Not Glenn's style. Excludes Last Word, Bijou, Naked & Famous, Final Ward, etc. Do not suggest as a purchase.

disliked_ingredients.name: Egg
disliked_ingredients.notes: No egg-based cocktails. Excludes flips, egg-white sours (Pisco Sour, Clover Club, Boston Sour, Whiskey Sour with foam, Ramos Gin Fizz, etc.), and any whole-egg or egg-white build. Eggs may be in the kitchen for cooking but are not used in drinks.

substitute_for_now.notes: Glenn enjoys tequila and plans to add it eventually. For citrus-forward shaken drinks (Margarita, Sidecar variants), the mezcal sub is excellent. For refresher-style cocktails (Paloma, Tommy's Margarita), the mezcal sub changes character meaningfully Ã¢ÂÂ flag the trade-off when relevant.

profile_evolution_log.reason: Migration from legacy single-file architecture to new repo structure (Barkeeper Bjorn v1.0).
